fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 13-year-old male brought by mother after expressing that he wishes he were 'never born' due to learning challenges.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 13-year-old male recently diagnosed with dysgraphia and ADHD presenting after telling mother he wishes he were never born because school is too hard. He endorses passive thoughts of not wanting to exist but denies plan, method, or intent. Reports feeling stupid despite average intelligence testing. Recently started receiving accommodations but embarrassed to use them. Sleep and appetite normal. Enjoys gaming and has online friends. Supportive mother. Currently on methylphenidate ER 27mg. No prior mental health concerns. (Social Worker Timothy Brooks, LCSW)

Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Passive suicidal ideation without plan or intent; academic frustration

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: ADHD; dysgraphia; adjustment disorder with depressed mood related to learning disabilities; passive suicidal ideation.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Recently diagnosed dysgraphia and ADHD; struggling academically; new accommodations but embarrassed to use; gaming interest; online friends; supportive mother.
Assessment_and_Plan: Low risk: passive SI without plan or intent in context of learning disability diagnosis.
Protective factors: mother support, gaming interest, online friends.
Normal reaction to learning disability diagnosis and academic struggles.
Counseling for learning differences and self-esteem.
School support to implement accommodations sensitively.
Connect with learning disability support groups.
Reframe accommodations as tools, not weakness.
Encourage gaming and online friendships as strengths.
Follow-up with therapist within one week.
Safe for discharge with appropriate support.
fields.Author: Timothy Brooks, LCSW (Social Worker)
